source_texts.title: Stakeholder Interview on the PROMISE Exercise Wallboard (revised)
source_texts.text:
Karen [Exercise Director]: Thanks for making time. The wallboard has to give us a live shared picture of what's happening and what's supposed to be happening, and it needs to be readable when we project it. People in the back of the room should immediately see what is happening now, even if they can’t read every line of text from that distance.

Luis [Operations Planner]: Agreed. The goal is to stop flipping through the MSEL and the sync matrix printouts. We keep saying one pane of glass—really a split view: graph and list side by side. I want the left two-thirds to be the visual timeline and the right third a vertical table sorted by time.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: And please, when we say timeline, it has to show both Events and Activities. I track things like Comm Check 3 and inject deliveries along with milestone moments. I don’t want just the headline events.

Devon [Data Manager]: I can bring in the MSEL Summary from the planning tools. Just to level set, the board will display the summary fields: title, planned start, planned duration, venue, and the key event flag. That keeps the wallboard consistent with what we already maintain.

Priya [IT Administrator]: Branding reminder: we must stick to the DHS color palette—no neon because it looks good on a projector. The UI and fonts have to meet our brand guidance.

Karen [Exercise Director]: I’m fine staying within the palette, but color needs to carry meaning. I want to know at a glance what’s late, on time, or early. If something drifts fifteen minutes behind, it should catch my eye—within the approved colors.

Luis [Operations Planner]: Layout-wise, left two-thirds is the timeline; right one-third is the time-ordered list. The right list should be split: top slice reserved for sequence-driven items we must do in order, and the lower portion focused on the items right around now.

Devon [Data Manager]: Exactly. Think of the top quarter as the sequence queue. The bottom three quarters shows items near the current time—things that just happened and what’s coming up next.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: In the heat of an exercise I want to glance and see the next few items in the next half hour and the last few that finished, plus a small, fixed area for the sequence tasks we follow regardless of exact timestamp.

Karen [Exercise Director]: And we need a strong indicator for now. When this is projected, people 30 feet back should be able to read the items, and from the very back of an auditorium you should still understand where we are on the schedule because the now indicator is unmistakable.

Priya [IT Administrator]: Let’s schedule a projector test for legibility—font sizes, contrast, and the now marker visibility. Also, the color contrast needs to meet accessibility guidance while staying inside the DHS palette.

Devon [Data Manager]: On the visuals, let’s put a clear now line on the timeline and maybe shade past vs future in the list. That should help people orient quickly.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: The timeline needs to lay things out by time in a way that makes longer activities and quick checkpoints clear enough. As long as it communicates timing cleanly, I’m happy.

Luis [Operations Planner]: And the list stays in ascending time order. The graph and the list should always reflect the same items so there’s no confusion.

Karen [Exercise Director]: We’ll also need filtering. Venue-based views are important—airport vs hospital, etc.—and sometimes I want only key events. Other times I want an all-events view, especially for hotwash to scan the whole day.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: It would also help to pick the time window or granularity—like 5, 15, or 30 minute grid—so density makes sense for what we’re doing.

Devon [Data Manager]: We can expose user-defined time segments and grid granularity. Users could define morning, lunch, afternoon, and choose the grid they want.

Priya [IT Administrator]: Mind performance while you do that. We’re targeting roughly a one-minute auto update. It should pull fresh data and repaint smoothly without feeling heavy.

Karen [Exercise Director]: And during briefings we sometimes want to freeze the screen. We need a Pause and a Resume so the board doesn’t shuffle while someone is talking, and it should be obvious when it’s paused.

Luis [Operations Planner]: Also, show both clocks: local time and exercise time. We almost always run an exercise clock that’s different than real time.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: Yes—people arrive late to injects if they follow their phone instead of the exercise clock.

Devon [Data Manager]: Data source-wise, we’ll pull from the current Sync Matrix 1.0 and the Exercise Management Tool 1.0. No double entry—no standalone spreadsheet.

Karen [Exercise Director]: If someone clicks an item on the wallboard, they should be able to jump to the source record to make edits in the original app. Deep links are fine; we’re not editing in the wallboard.

Priya [IT Administrator]: Deep links are okay, and the wallboard itself should be behind our normal sign-in. It should not show data to anyone who isn’t authorized internally. The source apps will enforce their own access when people follow links.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: That’s fine—as long as clicking opens the exact record in the source app, and those who have access there can make the change.

Luis [Operations Planner]: We’d like to tweak the display a bit on the fly—pick which columns show on the right list, hide a column if the screen is crowded. Nothing heavy; just session-level display adjustments.

Priya [IT Administrator]: Light customization is acceptable. Stay within brand and keep it simple for end users.

Karen [Exercise Director]: Reliability is important. During normal business hours when we’re running training, I want it up and not flaky. Let’s plan maintenance for off-hours. Early on, I’d like availability somewhere around 99 percent during those hours.

Devon [Data Manager]: On analytics, if we capture actual times, we can compare planned vs actual. A simple chart of actual vs planned will help in the after-action review.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: And a change history would help—seeing what changed and when during the day so we can explain any schedule drift.

Luis [Operations Planner]: Let’s keep export out of scope for now. Just an in-app history view is enough.

Priya [IT Administrator]: Agreed—no export in the first go. Also, to reiterate on security, the wallboard should only display to signed-in, authorized staff.

Karen [Exercise Director]: Training-wise, we don’t have time for long courses. New users should be able to launch the board and find what they need well under two hours the first time they touch it.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: Make it obvious—big play/pause for auto-refresh, clear venue and key-event filters. The rest is straightforward.

Devon [Data Manager]: Default refresh at 60 seconds sounds good. Pause should suspend updates and Resume should snap everything back to current exercise time.

Luis [Operations Planner]: On views, can we make All Events the default, then let people pick a venue and toggle key events?

Karen [Exercise Director]: Yes, and keep the timeline and list synchronized. If the exercise clock says 11:15, both panels should reflect that.

Priya [IT Administrator]: Label the two clocks clearly—local time and exercise time.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: One more thing: the ahead/behind indicator needs to use the exercise clock as the reference for planned vs actual, not local time.

Devon [Data Manager]: Right—the variance calculation should key off the exercise time.

Luis [Operations Planner]: About time segments, let us name them—morning, lunch, afternoon—and switch the view accordingly.

Karen [Exercise Director]: Works for me. And for readability, plan a projector test so we confirm most people can read it from about 30 feet, and that the now indicator is unmistakable even farther back.

Priya [IT Administrator]: I’ll book that test. Also, let’s integrate only with Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 for the first release. We’ll verify the deep-link routes for those.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: That’s the set we use now, right? Good.

Karen [Exercise Director]: On uptime, if we’re running nine to five, the board should keep itself current on about a one-minute cadence—no one should have to babysit the refresh.

Priya [IT Administrator]: The app should handle that cadence smoothly.

Luis [Operations Planner]: On colors, strong signals help. If the approved tones aren’t super bright, we can combine color with a small icon or label so late items stand out within the palette.

Devon [Data Manager]: I’ll draft the concept: left-right split, top quarter on the right for sequence items, bottom area near-now items. All driven by MSEL Summary, with references back to source IDs.

Karen [Exercise Director]: Don’t forget the key events toggle. Leadership likes a clean view.

Luis [Operations Planner]: All-events by default, venue views available, key events as a toggle—got it.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: And please include the simple actual-vs-planned chart for after action. In-app is fine.

Priya [IT Administrator]: That can be a separate tab or panel, read-only.

Devon [Data Manager]: For traceability, we’ll present a changes report listing what shifted in the event/activity data the board consumes—timestamps and who made the change when that information is provided by the sources.

Karen [Exercise Director]: Perfect—that will help explain schedule drift.

Luis [Operations Planner]: I’ll sketch the prototype with the dual-pane layout and the two clocks. We’ll try the Pause control in the briefing room.

Mia [Observer/Controller]: One more on terminology: people kept saying MDI, but we just mean a split view in one window. As long as it’s left-right, I’m good.

Priya [IT Administrator]: Yes—single window, split into panes. No pop-ups.

Devon [Data Manager]: I’ll document the field mapping from Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 and confirm the deep links. And note that links will only work once you’re signed in to the source apps.

Karen [Exercise Director]: Let’s lock the refresh at roughly every 60 seconds with a clear paused state when paused. That should prevent confusion.

Luis [Operations Planner]: Sounds good. Let’s regroup after the projector test.

source_texts.title: Working Session Notes — Layout, Integrations, and Ops Constraints (revised)
source_texts.text:
Date: 2025-03-18
Location: Conference room B / Teams link: https://agency.example/meet/Wallboard-Design-Session
Attendees: Karen (Exercise Director), Luis (Operations Planner), Mia (O/C), Devon (Data Manager), Priya (IT Admin), Sam (Vendor Lead) [joined late]

Notes (raw, incomplete):

- Goals recap: shared near-real-time exercise view; unify timelines from current planning tools; highlight schedule drift; support after-action with analytics and history; comply with DHS branding.

- Display concept: split workspace dual-pane wallboard. Left larger panel = timeline; right panel = vertical table (time-ordered). Left ~2/3, right ~1/3.

- Right-side table split: top quarter reserved for sequence-driven items (scripted steps we follow in order); lower three quarters focused on items near the current moment (recently completed and upcoming). Proximity window defaults around +/- 30 minutes [TBD if adjustable].

- Table columns (keep minimal): Title, Venue, Planned time, Actual (if available), variance indicator, link to source. Allow limited column show/hide.

- Timeline: show items along time with a clear "now" indicator. Consider shading for past region. Use color to reflect variance (ahead/on-time/behind) within DHS palette. Include a small legend for meaning.

- Variance: compute against exercise time, not local time. Keep this consistent in both panels.

- Two clocks on screen: local time and exercise time. Label clearly.

- Auto refresh: update about once per minute (target 60 sec). Avoid heavy flicker. Provide Pause/Resume; when paused, visibly indicate paused state; on resume, sync to current exercise time.

- Responsiveness: keep minute-level updates snappy. Keep UI navigations smooth.

- Filters/views: venue filter, Key Events toggle, and an All Events view. Add user-defined time segments (e.g., morning/lunch/afternoon) and timeline granularity options (5/15/30 minutes). Users can hide/show select columns. No schema changes; stay within brand.

- Data sources: only Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool (EMT) 1.0. Wallboard is a read-only display of MSEL Summary fields (title, planned start/duration, venue, key event flag, etc.).

- Deep links: each list row and timeline item should let users jump to the exact source record in Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0 for edits there. The wallboard itself does not edit data; links hand off to the source app which enforces its own access.

- Access: the wallboard should require sign-in and must not display data to unauthorized users.

- Availability/Uptime: Target near 99 percent during normal training/business hours in the initial ops period. Schedule maintenance off-hours.

- Training/Adoption: Intuitive UI. Expect most new users to get the board up and displaying Events/Activities in well under two hours on first use. Provide a quick start guide.

- Projection/readability: Design for large-screen projection. Test on a big screen. Aim for ~30 ft readability of key info for most viewers; ensure the "now" state is obvious from farther back even if text isn't fully readable.

- Analytics/Traceability: Provide a simple chart comparing actual vs planned times. Offer a change history view listing modifications to data the wallboard consumes (timestamps and, where provided by sources, who/what changed). In-app only (no export in v1).

- Timing rule: variance indicator uses exercise time; display both clocks to reduce confusion.

- Update cadence: default 60 sec polling. Pause/Resume present. Consider a subtle banner/icon to show paused state.

- Links back to source: links must open the exact item (by ID/GUID as appropriate) in the source app.

- Stateful Pause: when paused, the list and timeline remain fixed; Resume re-synchronizes to current exercise time.

- Views: All-Events view plus per-venue views, and a Key Events-only view.

- Terminology: use agency terms consistently—Event/Activity, Venue, Key Event, Variance (ahead/behind schedule). Provide a small legend.

- Split panes: one window with two regions; avoid pop-ups.

Contradictory/unclear items captured:
- Strong color signals vs brand limits — resolution: use DHS-approved tones with a legend; may add a small icon/label to reinforce meaning.
- Update every 60 sec vs real-time push — decision for v1: 60 sec polling; revisit later.
- Pause allowed vs risk of stale info — mitigate with a clear Paused indicator.
- Readability at 30 ft vs 100 ft — text readable at ~30 ft; at ~100 ft the "now" state should still be obvious; not all text must be readable at 100 ft.

Action items:
- Priya to circulate DHS palette and branding guidelines.
- Devon to define MSEL Summary fields and map from Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0.
- Sam to confirm deep-link patterns and demo a link opening the exact record.
- Karen to schedule projector readability test (big screen).
- Luis to draft a quick start (open board, apply filters, read clocks, pause/resume).
- Priya to draft an availability/maintenance window note (target near 99 percent during business hours; updates off-hours).
- Devon to propose the actual-vs-planned chart and a change history view format.

Parking Lot / Open:
- Whether the proximity window for the lower list is fixed or user-adjustable in v1.
- Saving custom display presets (not in v1).
- Auto-scroll behavior of the right-side list (check accessibility).

source_texts.title: Email Thread: Refresh Interval, Branding, Security, and Integration Details (revised)
source_texts.text:
Email 1
From: Karen Smith <karen.smith@agency.gov>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@agency.gov>; Luis Ortega <luis.ortega@agency.gov>; Devon Pike <devon.pike@agency.gov>
CC: Mia Chen <mia.chen@agency.gov>
Subject: PROMISE Wallboard - confirm refresh behavior and projection test

Hi all,

Quick follow-up. I want to lock in that the wallboard updates itself roughly every minute without anyone clicking refresh, and that we can pause it during a briefing and then resume. It should be obvious when it’s paused. Also, since we plan to project this on the big screen, can we schedule a legibility test next week? I want to validate that most of the room can read the event/activity fields at about 30 feet, and at least understand what is happening now even from the back rows.

Thanks,
Karen

—

Email 2
From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@agency.gov>
To: Karen Smith <karen.smith@agency.gov>
CC: Luis Ortega <luis.ortega@agency.gov>; Devon Pike <devon.pike@agency.gov>; Mia Chen <mia.chen@agency.gov>
Subject: RE: PROMISE Wallboard - confirm refresh behavior and projection test

Hi Karen,

Yes on the cadence: we’re aiming for a 60-second auto-refresh. We’ll add a Pause/Resume control with a clear indicator so people know it’s intentionally paused. We’ll also make sure the repaint is smooth.

On branding: we must stay within the DHS color palette. Some mockups used traffic-light colors; we can reflect the same logic, but only with approved tones and fonts. I’ll send the palette PDF today.

I can book the room for the projector test next Wednesday at 1400. We’ll validate font sizes, contrast, and the visibility of the now indicator from the back of the room.

Lastly, reiterating access control: the wallboard will be behind our standard sign-in and must not show any data to people who aren’t authorized internally.

Regards,
Priya

—

Email 3
From: Luis Ortega <luis.ortega@agency.gov>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@agency.gov>; Karen Smith <karen.smith@agency.gov>
CC: Devon Pike <devon.pike@agency.gov>; Mia Chen <mia.chen@agency.gov>; Sam Patel <sam@vendor.io>
Subject: RE: PROMISE Wallboard - confirm refresh behavior and projection test (plus integration + views)

Thanks Priya. Adding a couple items we didn’t explicitly capture:

1) Data source: no new data entry in the wallboard. It should pull Events/Activities from Sync Matrix 1.0 and the EMT (current production versions). Display the MSEL Summary fields (title, planned start/duration, venue, key event flag, etc.).

2) Navigation: For any row or timeline item, we need to jump to the originating record in Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0 to make edits there. Deep links are fine; the source apps will handle their own sign-in.

3) Views/filters: We need an All Events view, venue-based filtering, and a toggle for key events only. Also, a simple display tweak (e.g., hide a column) helps when the screen is crowded.

4) Clocks: Show local time and exercise time simultaneously. The variance indicator (ahead/behind) should reference exercise time, not the machine clock.

5) Timeline/table: Left is the timeline. Right is a vertical table sorted by time; its top quarter holds sequence-driven items; the lower section focuses on items near now.

Sam, can you confirm the deep-link patterns for both apps so we can open the exact item by ID?

-Luis

—

Email 4
From: Sam Patel <sam@vendor.io>
To: Luis Ortega <luis.ortega@agency.gov>
CC: Karen Smith <karen.smith@agency.gov>; Priya Shah <priya.shah@agency.gov>; Devon Pike <devon.pike@agency.gov>; Mia Chen <mia.chen@agency.gov>
Subject: RE: PROMISE Wallboard - confirm refresh behavior and projection test (plus integration + views)

Hi all,

On deep links: for Sync Matrix 1.0, we can open items by numeric ID at a route like sm/item/{id}. For EMT 1.0, the detail view opens with a GUID. We’ll map the MSEL Summary rows to their source IDs so the wallboard can construct the correct link. The wallboard itself will not allow edits; clicking hands off to the respective app (which will prompt for sign-in if the user isn’t already authenticated).

On refresh: 60-second polling is straightforward. We’ll add Pause/Resume. When paused, we’ll show a clear paused state and suspend polling. Resuming will fetch the latest and re-sync the time-based views with exercise time.

On analytics: we’ll include a basic chart view for actual vs planned times of Activities/Events. For change history, we’ll surface history from the source apps where available; otherwise we can present a history based on the changes the wallboard observes in the data it consumes. No export in the first release.

Branding: we’ll apply your DHS palette for variance colors and overall UI. We’ll also ensure the two clocks are labeled and visible.

Regards,
Sam

—

Email 5
From: Mia Chen <mia.chen@agency.gov>
To: Sam Patel <sam@vendor.io>; Luis Ortega <luis.ortega@agency.gov>
CC: Karen Smith <karen.smith@agency.gov>; Priya Shah <priya.shah@agency.gov>; Devon Pike <devon.pike@agency.gov>
Subject: RE: PROMISE Wallboard - observer/controller perspective

Hi team,

From the O/C side:

- Right-side list should be vertical and time sorted; the top quarter is the sequence list (step 1, step 2), and the lower portion focuses on items nearest to now. If the proximity window is adjustable later, great; not critical for v1.

- Make the variance color or symbol clear enough that late items stand out at a glance. Use the approved colors.

- Filters: venue, a key events toggle, and an all-events view are must-haves so I can switch contexts quickly.

- Pause during briefings is important; with a visible pause banner and resume control, that should stop questions about the board changing mid-sentence.

- I’ll be recording actual times as we go; seeing those reflected on the board within about a minute is the goal. The actual vs planned chart afterward will help with hotwash.

Thanks,
Mia

—

Email 6
From: Priya Shah <priya.shah@agency.gov>
To: All
Subject: RE: PROMISE Wallboard - availability, security, and scope

Adding a few constraints and one piece of pushback:

- Availability: during normal training/business hours, we need the wallboard available with minimal downtime. Initial target is near 99 percent availability. Maintenance will be scheduled off-hours.

- Security: the product must ensure that only authorized personnel can access it. No anonymous access.

- Training: we don’t have bandwidth for long courses. Expectation is that most new users can get the board up and displaying Events/Activities in well under two hours. A concise quick start helps.

- Scope pushback: let’s not add extra notifications or new integrations beyond Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0 for the first release.

Priya

—

Email 7
From: Devon Pike <devon.pike@agency.gov>
To: Priya Shah <priya.shah@agency.gov>; Sam Patel <sam@vendor.io>
CC: Karen Smith <karen.smith@agency.gov>; Luis Ortega <luis.ortega@agency.gov>; Mia Chen <mia.chen@agency.gov>
Subject: RE: PROMISE Wallboard - field mapping and change history details

All,

Summary of field mapping and a few design decisions:

- MSEL Summary fields to display: Title, Venue, Planned Start (exercise time), Planned Duration, Key Event flag, Source App (Sync Matrix 1.0 or EMT 1.0), Source ID, Actual Start (if recorded), Variance (computed as actual vs planned using exercise time). Each item includes a link target for deep linking.

- Variance colors will use the DHS palette. We can add a small text label (Ahead / On Time / Behind) in the legend to reinforce meaning.

- Two clocks on the top bar (local time and exercise time). The timeline’s now indicator aligns to the exercise clock.

- Display adjustments: allow hide/show of specific columns in the right table and let users define time segment boundaries (e.g., Morning = 0800–1200). Timeline granularity options: 5, 15, 30 minutes. We’re not saving presets in v1.

- Auto-refresh at 60 seconds by default. Pause/Resume implemented as a toggle; when paused, polling stops and a Paused ribbon shows. On resume, we fetch current data and align views to the exercise time.

- Availability: plan updates for off-hours. We’ll include a short note about maintenance windows and target availability.

- Change history: if the source apps expose change logs for fields we display, we’ll surface those in a History view. If not, we’ll present a history based on differences detected over time in the data the wallboard ingests (including timestamps and, where provided by sources, the user who made the change). In-app view only—no export in v1.

- Security: the wallboard will prompt for sign-in if the session expires. Deep links won’t bypass source app security.

Open items:

- Exact font sizes/contrast for projector readability—test Wednesday.
- Whether the proximity window for the lower list is fixed +/- 30 min or user-adjustable in v1 (leaning fixed for now).

Regards,
Devon

gold_requirements.text: The system shall refresh the display every 60 seconds.
The application shall match the color of the schema set forth by Department of Homeland Security
If projected the data must be readable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to read Event / Activity data from a viewing distance of 30
The product shall be available during normal business hours. As long as the user has access to the client PC the system will be available 99% of the time during the first six months of operation.

If projected the data must be understandable. On a 10x10 projection screen 90% of viewers must be able to determine that Events or Activities are occuring in current time from a viewing distance of 100
The product shall ensure that it can only be accessed by authorized users. The product will be able to distinguish between authorized and unauthorized users in all access attempts
The product shall be intuitive and self-explanatory. : 90% of new users shall be able to start the display of Events or Activities within 90 minutes of using the product.
The product shall respond fast to keep up-to-date data in the display.
The system shall have a MDI form that allows for the viewing of the graph and the data table.
The system shall display Events in a vertical table by time.
The system shall display the Events in a graph by time.
The system shall display Events or Activities.
The display shall have two regions: left 2/3 of the display is graphical right 1/3 of the display is a data table
The data displayed in both the nodes within the graph and the rows in the table are MSEL Summary data
The table side of the display shall be split into 2 regions: sequential and temporal.
The top 1/4 of the table will hold events that are to occur sequentially.
The bottom 3/4 of the table will hold events that occur according to its relevance to current time.
The system shall color code events according to their variance from current time.
The system shall display data from the Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0 applications
The system shall link Events back to either the Sync Matrix 1.0 or the Exercise Managment Tool 1.0 applications for modifications.
The system shall allow modification of the display.
The system shall offer a display of all the Events in the exercise.
The system shall filter data by: Venues and Key Events.
The system shall allow a user to define the time segments
The system shall display the local and exercise time in separate clocks
The system shall offer the ability to pause and resume the refresh of data.
The system shall provide charts for the Activity or Event actual versus assumed time.
The system shall provide a history report of changes made to the Activity or Event data

evidence_snippets: we’re aiming for a 60-second auto-refresh
coverage_entries.reason: Sources explicitly require ~60 second auto-refresh/polling.

evidence_snippets: stick to the DHS color palette
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple artifacts mandate using the DHS color palette.

evidence_snippets: read the event/activity fields at about 30 feet
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require projection legibility at ~30 ft for most viewers.

evidence_snippets: near 99 percent during normal training/business hours
coverage_entries.reason: Documents specify target ~99% availability during normal business hours.

evidence_snippets: at ~100 ft the 'now' state should still be obvious
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require the now indicator to be obvious from far back.

evidence_snippets: must not show data to people who aren’t authorized internally
coverage_entries.reason: Wallboard must require sign-in and prevent unauthorized data viewing.

evidence_snippets: get the board up ... in well under two hours
coverage_entries.reason: Documents expect intuitive UI and quick setup by new users.

evidence_snippets: keep minute-level updates snappy
coverage_entries.reason: Responsiveness and smooth minute-level updates are explicitly required.

evidence_snippets: split workspace dual-pane wallboard
coverage_entries.reason: Sources specify a split view showing timeline (graph) and table.

evidence_snippets: right panel = vertical table (time-ordered)
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple notes require a vertical, time-ordered right-side table.

evidence_snippets: split view: graph and list side by side
coverage_entries.reason: Interview and notes require a time-based graph/timeline.

evidence_snippets: show both Events and Activities
coverage_entries.reason: Text explicitly states timeline must show Events and Activities.

evidence_snippets: left two-thirds timeline; right one-third table
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple sources specify left 2/3 graphical, right 1/3 table.

evidence_snippets: display the MSEL Summary fields
coverage_entries.reason: Devon and notes state wallboard shows MSEL Summary data fields.

evidence_snippets: Right-side table split: sequential and temporal
coverage_entries.reason: Notes and interview define table split into sequence and temporal.

evidence_snippets: top quarter reserved for sequence-driven items
coverage_entries.reason: Meeting notes explicitly assign top 1/4 to sequence items.

evidence_snippets: bottom 3/4 shows items near current time
coverage_entries.reason: Sources describe lower three-quarters showing near-now relevant items.

evidence_snippets: Use color to reflect variance (ahead/on-time/behind)
coverage_entries.reason: Stakeholders require color coding for schedule variance within palette.

evidence_snippets: pull from Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0
coverage_entries.reason: Documents state data sources are Sync Matrix 1.0 and EMT 1.0.

evidence_snippets: items should link back to the exact source record
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple artifacts require deep links to source apps for edits.

evidence_snippets: pick which columns show
Allow limited column show/hide
coverage_entries.reason: Users can tweak/hide columns and perform light display customization.

evidence_snippets: All Events view
display of all the Events in the exercise
coverage_entries.reason: Sources explicitly request an All-Events view showing all events.

evidence_snippets: venue filter, Key Events toggle
Views: per-venue views, Key Events-only view
coverage_entries.reason: Specifies venue filtering and a Key Events toggle/filter.

evidence_snippets: user-defined time segments
Users could define morning, lunch, afternoon
coverage_entries.reason: Documents user-defined time segments and configurable grid granularity.

evidence_snippets: two clocks on screen: local time and exercise time
Show local time and exercise time simultaneously
coverage_entries.reason: Multiple sources require separate local and exercise clocks.

evidence_snippets: Pause and Resume
Pause/Resume control with a clear indicator
coverage_entries.reason: Sources require pausing/resuming auto-refresh with visible paused state.

evidence_snippets: simple chart comparing actual vs planned times
include a basic chart view for actual vs planned times
coverage_entries.reason: Chart for actual versus planned times is explicitly requested.

evidence_snippets: change history view listing modifications
surface history from the source apps
coverage_entries.reason: Sources request a change history report of observed/source changes.

unsupported_implied.text: The timeline shall offer selectable grid granularity (e.g., 5, 15, 30 minute intervals) so users can change how time is binned/displayed.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "pick the time window or granularity—like 5, 15, or 30 minute grid"
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold set requires allowing user-defined time segments but does not require adjustable timeline grid/granularity options. The source explicitly asks for selectable 5/15/30 minute granularity, which is a distinct UI capability not covered by the gold requirements.

unsupported_implied.text: The application shall be a single-window split-pane UI only (no pop-ups) — i.e., a single-window MDI with two fixed regions (left/right) and no separate pop-up windows.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "one window with two regions; avoid pop-ups."
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold requires an MDI form showing graph and table but does not forbid pop-ups or require a single-window-only constraint. The source explicitly mandates a single-window split view and forbids pop-ups, which is an additional UI constraint not present in the gold set.

unsupported_implied.text: Pause must present a clear visible paused state (e.g., a ribbon/banner) and suspend polling; Resume must fetch the latest data and re-synchronize the views to current exercise time.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "Pause/Resume; when paused, visibly indicate paused state; on resume, sync to current exercise time."
unsupported_implied.reason: While the gold requires the ability to pause and resume refresh, it does not require a clearly visible paused indicator nor specify that resume must re-fetch and align the display to exercise time. The source explicitly requires both the visible paused state and the resume re-synchronization behavior, which are concrete functional behaviors beyond the gold wording.

unsupported_implied.text: The lower (near-now) portion of the right-side table shall present items within a defined proximity window by default (approximately +/-30 minutes) for the 'near now' view.
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "Proximity window defaults around +/- 30 minutes [TBD if adjustable]."
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold requires the table split into sequential and temporal regions and that the bottom 3/4 hold near-current items, but it does not specify a default proximity window size. The source specifies a concrete default of +/-30 minutes for the proximity window, which is an operational requirement absent from the gold set.

unsupported_implied.text: The change history view shall include timestamps and, where the source provides it, the identity of who made the change (i.e., surface actor metadata when available).
unsupported_implied.source_snippet: "change history would help—seeing what changed and when during the day" / "timestamps and who made the change when that information is provided by the sources."
unsupported_implied.reason: The gold requires a history report of changes but does not require inclusion of actor/identity metadata. The source explicitly requires showing who made changes when that information is available, which is an additional traceability detail not covered by the gold requirements.

brief.domain: emergency management training and exercise control
stakeholders: Department of Homeland Security program office
business_goals: Provide a shared real-time view of exercise events and activities to improve coordination
Unify timelines from existing planning tools to eliminate double entry and keep data consistent
Enable monitoring of timing performance during the exercise and highlight schedule drift
Support after-action review with timing analytics and a full change audit trail
Operate securely and reliably during training hours while complying with agency branding
core_features: Dual-pane wallboard (MDI workspace): left two-thirds as a graphical timeline, right one-third as a time-ordered table
Right-side table split: top quarter for sequence-driven items, lower three-quarters for items relative to ‘now’; vertical timeline layout
Color-coded indicators showing how far each event/activity is ahead or behind current time
Integration with Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0, with deep links to open items in the source apps for edits
Filters and views: by venue and key events, plus an all-events view; user-defined time segments and customizable display
Time and refresh controls: separate clocks for local and exercise time; ability to pause/resume auto-refresh; minute-level auto-update
Analytics and traceability: actual-vs-planned timing charts and a change history report
quality_concerns: Responsive updates with the display auto-refreshing roughly every 60 seconds
High uptime during normal business hours (near 99% target in the initial period)
Access restricted to authorized personnel with role-based sign-in
Low training burden so new users can get the board running quickly
Visual compliance with DHS color palette/branding
Readable and clear when projected on a large screen, including clear indication of what is happening now from the back of the room
constraints: Must follow DHS branding and color standards
Runs on standard client PCs in EOC/classroom environments; no special hardware
Integrates specifically with Sync Matrix 1.0 and Exercise Management Tool 1.0
Access limited to authorized users per agency security policy
Intended for normal training/business hours; maintenance and updates scheduled off-hours
